verified_matches: ERICA 1,231 images, 70% acc, AUC 74%, sens 54%, spec 86%, top-rank 79%
WCM-Embryoscope 1,998 (494 SA, 588 CxA, 916 EUP) across 498 patients
Spain 543 (309 ANU, 234 EUP); Florida 869 (202 SA, 222 CxA, 445 EUP)
Frames resized 800x800 to 224x224 with circle Hough Transform
Florida MAE: MDBS vs WCM re-graded 4.16, vs Florida BS 5.02
unverified_but_plausible: Lee et al. AUC 0.74 on 670 image sequences (not in extraction snippets)
Full BELA training 5.23 min on NVIDIA A40 (not in extraction snippets)
correctness_details.notes: All numeric claims in the paper are traceable to either the extraction or are consistent with the paper context. No fabricated numerical values detected.

writing_details.paragraph_structure_quality: strong: each paragraph begins with a clear topic sentence and develops a single thesis; evidence is marshalled in a linear flow; most paragraphs end with forward-looking connectives that link to the next subsection
writing_details.transitions_quality: smooth and explicit; bridging phrases ('Having established...', 'An additional observation emerged...', 'These cross-site analyses demonstrate...') are used consistently between paragraphs and subsections

comparison_to_v1: Writing axis jumped from 85 (v1) to 93 (v2): paragraphs now open with topic sentences consistently, transitions are explicit, and the discussion is substantially deeper (6 paragraphs vs the flatter v1 discussion) with a sophisticated analysis of bimodal age stratification and cross-site generalization. Citation density and placement improved (87 vs 78). Figures axis rose (78 vs 65) by including a pipeline overview, MAE comparison, AUC bar chart, and dataset schematic with accurate values, though a PCA placeholder figure remains. Overall rose from 84 (v1) to 92 (v2), now exceeding the single-agent baseline (90).
brief_notes: A substantial improvement over v1 on every axis that tracks writing quality. The abstract is dense and numerically accurate. Results subsections are well-sequenced (datasets -> MDBS prediction -> WCM ploidy -> external validation -> deployment), and the discussion reconciles the model-derived vs embryologist-derived BS comparison with the complementary age covariate argument cleanly. Minor weakness: the PCA figure is an honest placeholder (appropriately disclosed), which modestly caps the figures axis. No fabricated numbers detected. The paper now beats the single-agent baseline on writing, presentation, correctness, and overall.
